title: Barn Door Entertainment Center
summary: A wide 1x12 media cabinet with a center TV bay and two side towers, closed off by a full-width sliding barn door — heavier and more wall-dependent than this catalog's barn-door bookcase.
description:
This entertainment center adapts this catalog's barn-door bookcase idea to a media cabinet's proportions: a wide center bay sized for a TV (or extra shelves if you're skipping one), flanked by two shelved towers, all behind one continuous sliding barn door. Build the case frame first with pocket holes, hiding them on the insides, then decide the media bay's internal layout before adding shelves — the plan's own fallback for a no-TV setup is simply more shelves in that bay, so this decision should happen before shelving goes in, not after.

Mark the location of every fixed shelf on the sides before the back panel goes on, then use those side marks to draw reference lines across the back for nailing the shelves in the right place — doing the layout on the sides first, while they're still separate from the back, is more accurate than trying to mark shelf height on an installed back panel.

**This door is genuinely heavy, and the plan is explicit about it**: don't depend on the thin back panel to anchor the whole project to the wall. Add a cleat at the top or use L-brackets specifically for the wall connection — a media cabinet with a sliding door and a TV inside it is meaningfully top-heavy, and the back panel alone isn't rated to carry that anchoring load. Build the door to fit whatever hardware you buy, since barn door track kits vary in exact door-thickness requirements between manufacturers.

tools.note: The angled cross-support cuts on the door.

materials.name: Barn door track hardware kit, 8 ft
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the case frame
steps.body: Build the frame of the sliding-door cabinet box using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, hiding pocket holes on the insides.

steps.title: Add the media bay layout
steps.body: Add the media unit shelf and divider to the center bay. For no TV, or a TV that doesn't fill the space, add additional shelves instead.

steps.title: Add the tower shelves
steps.body: Add the remaining shelves in the side towers using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Mark the location of all fixed shelves on the sides first, then place the plywood back panels with glue and draw reference lines between the side marks to locate the shelves. Nail or staple with 3/4" fasteners. The center back panel should land at the center of the 1x12 sides.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Nail and glue the face frame onto the front of the case.

steps.title: Build the barn door
steps.body: Build the door to fit your specific barn door hardware kit's thickness requirement. For cross-support pieces, build the main door frame with pocket holes first, then set the cross piece in place uncut, trace the angles, and cut with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Hang the door and anchor to the wall
steps.body: Attach the door with the hardware kit. Because of the door's weight, securely anchor the entire project to the wall with L-brackets or a top cleat — do not depend on the thin plywood back to hold the piece to the wall. Sand and finish.
materials: Barn door track hardware kit, 8 ft
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Wide media cabinet with a sliding barn door covering a TV bay and side towers
